concerns.text: The plan cites 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/nateraw/dummy-csv-dataset/resolve/main/data.csv' as the source for NOAA Kp/Dst indices. This is a dummy dataset and does not contain the required geomagnetic time-series data (Kp, Dst). The plan explicitly acknowledges 'NO verified source found' for real NOAA data and proposes using synthetic data for the CI run. However, the specification (FR-001, FR-006) and the research goal require a 'reproducible, end-to-end pipeline' using real ACE and NOAA data. Relying on synthetic data for the primary analysis logic invalidates the scientific claim of quantifying correlations between real solar wind and geomagnetic indices. The pipeline cannot fulfill the research question with the cited dataset.

The plan states: 'The pipeline will include a --noaa-source argument... For the CI run, it will use a synthetic generator... while logging a warning that real data is missing.' This approach fails the Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) and Principle II (Verified Accuracy) because the 'reproducible' run does not use the canonical real-world data required to answer the research question. A pipeline that runs on synthetic data to 'verify logic' but cannot run on real data due to missing verified sources is not a valid implementation of the spec's requirement to download and analyze real NOAA indices.
